name: Pandera
pricing_notes: Fully open source (MIT); no API costs

auth.notes: Pure Python library; no auth required

performance.notes: Validation overhead minimal for small-medium DataFrames; lazy validation option for large DataFrames

agent_readiness.notes: Statistical validation of DataFrames (pandas, polars, Dask, Spark). Schema declaration via Python classes or decorators. Validates column types, value ranges, uniqueness, nullability. Works at function boundaries via @check_types decorator. Hypothesis integration for property-based schema testing. Excellent for data pipeline input/output validation.

gotchas: Validation overhead scales with DataFrame size — use lazy mode for large DataFrames
Complex cross-column constraints can be verbose to express
Polars/Dask/Spark support lags behind pandas (main target)
Learning curve for advanced schema definition patterns
best_when: Data pipeline input/output validation with descriptive error messages
Type-annotated DataFrame functions requiring runtime validation
ML feature engineering pipelines needing schema contracts
Agents generating DataFrames that need structural validation before use
avoid_when: Performance-critical paths with very large DataFrames (use lazy mode or avoid)
summary: Pandera is a statistical DataFrame validation library for pandas, polars, Dask, and Spark. Declare schemas via Python classes or decorators, validate column types/ranges/nullability at runtime. @check_types decorator validates function inputs/outputs automatically. Excellent error messages. Best for data pipeline validation contracts and ML feature engineering quality gates.
